headline: Everyone Thinks Iran Will Be Another Forever War—Except Defense Stocks
summary: Defense stocks fell again Tuesday, failing to get a boost from ongoing fighting in the Middle East, which may or may not be abating soon.  Shares of  Lockheed Martin  and  Northrop Grumman  both fell almost 2% on Tuesday, while the  and  lost 0.2% and 0.1%, respectively.  Tuesday’s declines left Lockheed and AeroVironment shares down about 1% and 12% since the fighting in Iran began.

headline: Defense Stocks Are Down. Why the Iran War Hasn’t Given Them a Lift.
summary: Defense stocks fell again Tuesday, failing to get a boost from ongoing fighting in the Middle East, which may or may not be abating soon.  Shares of  Lockheed Martin  and  Northrop Grumman  were both down more than 1% in early trading on Tuesday, while the  and  were both off about 0.4%.  Tuesday’s declines left Lockheed and AeroVironment shares down 0.4% and 11.6% since the fighting in Iran began.

headline: Why This Defense ETF Could Keep Rallying as the Iran Conflict Escalates
summary: The war with Iran is driving defense costs to $1 billion per day, and this ETF is likely to continue outperforming the S&P 500 as a result.

headline: RTX's Pratt & Whitney Scores Fresh Leidos Deal
summary: RTX's Pratt & Whitney secures a follow-on contract to supply TJ150 turbojet engines for Leidos' AGM-190A small cruise missiles.
